scope.summary: Implement the MetaHuman garment export package that bundles the promoted garment mesh with MetaHuman-specific skeletal binding data so MetaHuman Creator can re-size the garment automatically when body sliders are adjusted
acceptance_criteria: POST /tailor/garments/:id/export with format=metahuman produces a directory containing: (1) a USD or FBX mesh with MetaHuman-compatible skinning weights, (2) a MetaHuman import JSON manifest listing the garment slot, body region, and LOD paths, and (3) a seam-allowance-stripped OBJ of each 2D panel for MetaHuman Creator's pattern-resize algorithm

The export is deferred to post-v1 and returns HTTP 501 NOT_IMPLEMENTED with a documented roadmap note until the full implementation is available — the MT is the placeholder that notes the deferred requirement in T-PIPELINE-INTEROP
The ModelManual entry describes the deferred status and the recommended workaround (export USD + use the batch refit MT-313 across a size run approximating the MetaHuman body slider range)
MOAT-7 note is recorded in the T-PIPELINE-INTEROP spec section referencing this MT as the deferred implementation anchor
scope.risk_if_missed: Without this export path, garments authored in Tailor cannot round-trip to MetaHuman Creator for body-slider-driven resizing, requiring manual re-fit for each body variation. This is a critical gap for UE5 game and cinematic productions using MetaHuman as their character standard.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
